content.rendered:
Call for Applications: PGC Visayas Internship Program 2023

The Philippine Genome Center Visayas is calling for interns to join our Genomics Track and Bioinformatics Track. Our internships offer an exciting and challenging opportunity to work in a dynamic research environment. Our interns will be exposed to cutting-edge technologies and gain valuable skills and experience in genomics and bioinformatics. The interns will be involved in projects related to genomic sequencing, data analysis, and data management. This internship is an excellent opportunity to gain experience in the fields of genomics and bioinformatics.

Internship: Genomics Track

APPLICANT QUALIFICATIONS & SKILLS

Must be a an incoming Senior or Graduating BS student with a good academic record who are currently enrolled in a science-related program at a recognized college or university.

Must be an undergraduate student taking up courses such as BS Biology, BS Molecular Biology and Biotechnology.

Minimum of 6 units in biology, molecular biology, or a related field.

Eager to learn new things quickly.

Positive working attitude and show enthusiasm and motivation to perform.

Have excellent verbal, listening, and written communication skills in English.

REQUIREMENTS

  Motivational Letter from potential trainee addressed to:

               Victor Marco Emmanuel N. Ferriols, Ph.D., Program Director – Philippine Genome Center Visayas

  Letter of Recommendation from OJT Adviser

  Curriculum Vitae (in .pdf form)

SCHEDULE OF INTERNSHIP

June 1 to August 31 , 2023, 8 Weeks

Internship: Bioinformatics Track

APPLICANT QUALIFICATIONS & SKILLS

Must be a an incoming Senior or Graduating BS student with a good academic record who are currently enrolled in a science-related program at a recognized college or university.

Must be an undergraduate student taking up courses such as BS Computer Science or with background in Biology.

Has experience in at least one programming language, preferably familiar with the Unix/Linux command line environment.

Minimum of 3 units in biology, molecular biology, or a related field.

Eager to learn new things quickly.

Positive working attitude and show enthusiasm and motivation to perform.

Have excellent verbal, listening, and written communication skills in English.

REQUIREMENTS

  Motivational Letter from potential trainee addressed to:

               Victor Marco Emmanuel N. Ferriols, Ph.D., Program Director – Philippine Genome Center Visayas

  Letter of Recommendation from OJT Adviser

  Curriculum Vitae (in .pdf form)

SCHEDULE OF INTERNSHIP

July 10-August 14, 2023

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Where will the PGC Visayas Internship Program be held?

The PGC Visayas Internship Program will be held at the Philippine Genome Center Visayas which is located at the University of the Philippines Visayas Miagao campus in Miagao, Iloilo.

How can I verify that my online application has been received?

You will receive an email stating that your application has been received.

What is the duration of the internship program?

The duration of the Internship program will depend on the requirements of the program that the intern is enrolled to. PGC will offer internships annually from July to August.

Do I need to be a current student to apply?

Yes, the current internship program is designed for undergraduate students only.

What are the eligibility requirements for the internship?

Must be an incoming Senior or Graduating BS student with a good academic record who is currently enrolled in a science-related program at a recognized college or university.

 

For Genomics Track: Must be an undergraduate student taking up courses such as BS Biology, BS Molecular Biology and Biotechnology. The applicant must have a minimum of 6 units in biology, molecular biology, or any related field.

 

For Bioinformatics Track: Must be an undergraduate college student taking up courses such as BS Computer Science or with a background in Biology. The applicant must have an experience in at least one programming language, preferably familiar with the Unix/Linux command line environment

Do I need to be familiar with computer programming to apply for the Bioinformatics Track?

Yes, the bioinformatics focal area emphasizes writing custom scripts to analyze next generation sequencing data. Entering the intensive internship in bioinformatics with some programming experience will help you keep up with the fast pace delivery of the material.

What do I need to prepare to apply?

You will need to submit the following: Motivational Letter from potential trainee addressed to: Victor Marco Emmanuel N. Ferriols, Ph.D., Program Director – Philippine Genome Center Visayas; Letter of Recommendation from OJT Adviser; and Curriculum Vitae (in .pdf form).

Do we need to submit a recommendation letter?

To complete the application, the applicant must submit/request a Letter of Recommendation from the OJT Adviser. The Letter of Recommendation must be prepared by an OJT Adviser who is familiar with your scientific accomplishments, motivation, scientific interests, and skills. If the OJT adviser is not available, the curriculum adviser, program adviser, or department head may also write the recommendation letter. The Letter of Recommendation must be included in the email attachments that will be sent by the applicant.

Do you allow students to apply for internships even though they still have not taken the internship/OJT practicum as their subject load and/or the internship is not included in the curriculum, provided that the applicant has the desired qualifications?


We highly prioritize those applicants who are currently having Internship/OJT Practicum as their subject load and included in their curriculum, nonetheless, we will still accept their internship applications. The school or college department will still decide whether to credit the internship of their students who are taking an internship outside of their subject load.

Is the internship schedule flexible?

We are currently following a work plan and the activities are already scheduled for the whole duration of the internship. For special cases, we do offer a flexible schedule/time slot as long as the intern meets the required number of hours within the allotted period for the internship (June to August 2023).

Is there a minimum GPA I must have in order to apply to the program?

No. We look at the entire application and do not disqualify applicants based on a single feature.

When are applications reviewed?

All applications will be reviewed until March 30, 2023.

What are the relocation or travel expenses associated with the internship?

PGC Visayas is located at the University of the Philippines Visayas Miagao and is an hour away from Iloilo City. We recommend that the interns must secure a place to stay in Miagao for the whole duration of the internship.

content.rendered:
PGC Visayas Bioinformatics Laboratory

Clients can get accurate, and in-depth information of the sequencing data obtained from next-generation sequencing technologies generated and analyzed by computer programs.

 PGC Visayas Bioinformatics Laboratory servers are configured as an HPC Cluster with 120 cores or 240 threads, 640GB RAM and 360 Terabytes storage.

 PGC Visayas Bioinformatics Laboratory workstations are a 4 gigahertz CPU speed with 16 gigabyte memory and 1 terabyte HDD and 1 terabyte SSD storage.

Our facility’s analysis and training services includes DNA barcoding, Metagenomics, Transcriptomics, and Whole Genome Analysis, a custom workflow for the mentioned analysis above and a basic programming or bash scripting.

PGC Visayas Bioinformatics Laboratory Services

DNA Barcoding

About DNA Barcoding

DNA barcoding is a tool for rapid species identification based on DNA sequences. Efficiently recognize known species and speed up the discovery of new species.

SERVICES:

  • Rapid species identification
  • Identification of unknown material
  • Species authentication

Metagenomics

About Metagenomics

Metagenomics is the study of genomes contained within an entire microbial community. Metagenomic sequencing focuses on microbial community diversity analysis, gene composition and function, as well as metabolic pathways associated with the specific environment.

SERVICES:

  • Analysis of species composition and abundance
  • Genome components analysis
  • Generate non-redundant gene catalog
  • Gene functional annotations
  • Comparative analysis among samples

Transcriptomics

About Transcriptomics

The transcriptome is the complete set of transcripts in a cell, tissue, or whole organism, for a specific developmental stage or physiological condition.

Transcriptomic Data Analysis involves characterization of all transcriptional activity (coding and non-coding), or a select subset of RNA transcripts within a given sample. The analysis of transcriptomes allows the identification of candidate genes and expressed markers associated with traits of interest.

SERVICES:

  • Identification of the expressed genes
  • Determination of the gene structures
  • Quantification of absolute and relative gene expression levels
  • Differential expression analysis
  • Gene Ontology (GO) enrichment analysis
  • Target gene prediction and network/pathway analysis

Whole Genome Analysis

About Whole Genome Analysis

In Whole Genome Analysis, the de novo sequence can be used on characterized genomes if there is no available reference sequence or known genomes if significant variations are expected.

The general strategy of this analysis is to align and merge short fragments derived from a much longer DNA sequence in order to reconstruct the original sequence.

It usually takes multiple libraries and multiple rounds of finishing to get a complete genome sequence.

SERVICES:

  • Generation of high-quality reference genome assemblies
  • Structural and functional annotation of genes
  • Identification and phylogenetic analysis of gene families
  • Prediction of biosynthetic pathways
  • Gene annotation and prediction

Researchers from Mindoro State University Trained at PGC Visayas Bioinformatics Laboratory

content.rendered:
PGC VISAYAS HIGH SCHOOL ROADSHOW

 

The expansion of PGC to Visayas and Mindanao necessitates raising awareness on the relevance of genomics to the public.

In PGC Visayas, we introduced our facility to high school students by clarifying their ideas on what we will do.

The PGC Visayas team visited three high schools for the 2019 PGC VSF High School Roadshow last September to October 2019.

These high schools are University of the Philippines High School in Iloilo (UPHSI) (September 9, 2019), Philippine Science High School – MIMAROPA Region Campus (PSHS-MRC) (September 12, 2019) and Philippine Science High School – Western Visayas Campus (PSHS-WVC) (October 9, 2019).

content.rendered:
Frequently Asked Questions
Consortium Membership

How can an institution be a PGC Visayas consoritum member?

An institution can be a PGC Visayas consortium member of they have a memorandum of understanding (MOU) and memorandum of academic and research cooperation (MOARC) with UP Visayas. PGC Visayas consortium members are expected to submit a genomics-based proposal in 2 years.

How can research proposal be submitted?

Research proposals can be submitted through e-mail to pgc.upvisayas@up.edu.ph. Please address all communications oto the Program Director of PGC Visayas.

How will information be dissemincated about the activities of the PGC Visayas Capacity- Building Scheme?

PGC Visayas will dissemincate information about the activities of the PGC Visayas Capacity-Building Scheme by e-mail or phone call. The official PGC Visayas e-mail address is pgc.upvisayas@up.edu.ph.

How long will the PGC Visayas Capacity-Building Scheme last?

Meetings and research repacking seminars will be conducted as needed. The Bioinformatics and labs skills training components of the PGC Visayas Capacity-Building Scheme is projected to last for six (6) months. The basic Bioinformatics Seminar was conducted last August 30, 2019 followed by the Bioinformatics Workshop on Variant Calling, Population Structure and GWAS last October 14-16, 2019.

What is expected from PGC Visayas consortium members after undergoing the PGC Visayas
Capacity-Building Scheme?

PGC Visayas consortium members are expected to submit a sound genomic-based research proposal after undergoing the PGC Visayas Capacity-Building Scheme.

Who can participate in the activities in the
PGC Visayas Capacity-Building Scheme?

Personnel from PGC Visayas consortium members and PGC Visayas Project Staff can partificpate in the activities of the PGC Visayas Capacity-BUilding Scheme.

content.rendered:
Frequently Asked Questions
Services & Laboratory Use

Where is PGC Visayas located?

PGC Visayas is housed at the Regional Research Center (RRC), UP Visayas, Miagao, Iloilo. The RRC building is accessible through the road on the right side of the old Landbank Miagao Branch.

What forms will be needed in availing PGC Visayas Services?

Interested clients can fill up the following forms in availing PGC Visayas service: Client Information Sheet, Client Conforme, and Request for DNA Sequencing Services (Equipment Use), which are readily available at the laboratory and can also be downloaded through the PGC Visayas website.

What services does PGC Visayas offer?

Currently, PGC Visayas offers sample processing services, sequencing services, bioinformatics services, retail services, and equipment use (without consumables)

Can PGC Visayas consortium members skip some activities in the capacity-building scheme?

Yes, if they submit proposals that does not merit revisions from the PGC Visayas program director and project staff.

Can clients personally operate equipment PGC Visayas equipment?

Yes, commo equipment can be personally operated by the Clients, however some equipment in the laboratory can only be operated by trained staff and personnel. These can also be personally handled by Clients upon receiving a certificate of training from PGC Visayas.

Who can avail the services of PGC Visayas?

The Filipino people can avail the PGC Visayas services.

Can lab space be rented at PGC Visayas?

No, PGC Visayas only offers equipment use.

content.rendered:
Frequently Asked Questions
COVID-19

Do you perform swabbing or conduct RT-PCR testing for COVID-19?

No, we do not perform swabbing or conduct RRT-PCR testing for COVID-19.

Do you accept COVID-19 swab samples?

No, we do not accept COVID-19 samples.

Do you offer online training on molecular diagnostic technques?

No, we do not offer an online training for molecular diagnostic techniques.

How can I be a participant on molecular techniques training for COVID-19 RT-PCR?

Your institution should coordinate with the Department of Health (DOH) to undergo the “UP COLLab: COVID-19 Online Learning Lab for RT-PCR Testing” since all training conducted by PGC Visayas on molecular techniques is in coordination with the Department of Health (DOH).

You have to pass an assessment exam to be endorsed for the 2-day hands-on training program.

Who can I contact for the UP collab online training?

Requests for UP collab online training of Medical Technologist Certification for CoVID-19 should be emailed to:

  • Dr. Orleen Raymund C. Quilao of the Department of Health
  • Email address: ocquilao@doh.gov.ph

content.rendered:
Mandate

The formation of PGC Visayas and PGC Mindanao was approved through the amendment of the PGC organizational chart during the 1342nd BOR meeting held last March 1, 2019. Given the mandate of UP Visayas (which hosts PGC Visayas) to respond to the need to accelerate the development of fisheries and marine resources in the country, PGC Visayas is set to spearhead OMICS research in the field of fisheries, marine science, and allied disciplines. With the potential of such a facility strategically located in the region, PGC Visayas is not limited to the previously mentioned fields and shall strive to support research, development, and innovation in the fields of agriculture, health, and other allied disciplines as well. This is realized through partnerships of PGC Visayas and UPV with the various higher education institutions, research institutions, government agencies, non-government organizations, and other organizations and stakeholders throughout the Visayas and beyond.

Mission

The Philippine Genome Center is the country’s leading institution for omics-driven technologies and customized services.

We therefore aim to:

+ train and provide growth opportunities to our scientists, collaborators , and partners while embracing diversity and inclusivity;

+ commit to the highest level of ethical standards on institutional governance and financial management of allocated resources; and

+ contribute to the macroeconomic growth of the country by continuously seeking new knowledge and innovations in the field of multi-omics sciences.

Vision

To be the premiere institution on relevant translational multi-omics research and services in the country that is

responsive to the needs of the Filipinos and the society by 2025.

content.rendered:
How the Philippine Genome Center Visayas contributed to the fight against COVID19

Philippine Genome Center Visayas has contributed in the fight against COVID-19 in terms of provision of scientific advice, assistance to Manila HealthTek team and RITM, inventory of required materials for COVID-19 testing, loan of equipment and conduct of COVID-19 testing-related training. 

PGC Visayas was tapped by DOH-RITM as one of the institutions to be part of The UP COLLab: COVID-19 Online Learning Lab for RT PCR Testing (UCC) together with UPD NIMBB, PGC-Dil, and PGC Mindanao.

The UP COLLab: COVID-19 Online Learning Lab for RT PCR Testing is a weekly 2-day online course in the harmonized approach for the training of personnel of laboratories being set up for COVID-19 by DOH-RITM.

PGC Visayas held its first Training on Molecular Techniques (RNA Extraction and rRT- PCR) for COVID-19 Testing under DOH-RITM’s harmonized approach in UPV NIMBB Laboratories, College of Fisheries and Ocean Science, UP Visayas, Miagao Iloilo.

excerpt.rendered: How the Philippine Genome Center Visayas contributed to the fight against COVID19 Philippine Genome Center Visayas has contributed in the fight against COVID-19 in terms…

content.rendered:
HISTORY

In 2019, Philippine Genome Center Visayas Satellite Facility (PGC VSF) was established due to the need to expand Philippine Genome Center (PGC). The Philippine government wanted higher education institutions, hospitals and other research institutions to tap the agricultural, fisheries, medical and other allied sciences applications of high-end sequencers they invested in PGC. The establishment of PGC VSF is a major step in the advancement of genomics in the Philippines as preceded by the formal launching of PGC in 2011, PGC’s DNA Sequencing Core Facility (DSCF) launching in 2013 and PGC’s Core Facility for Bioinformatics (CFB) in 2014.

            Discussions on the establishment of PGC VSF started as early as October 2017 during the “Partnering for Genomics Research, Collaborating for Capacity Building” seminar organized by PGC in Iloilo Convention Center. A series of video chats followed this seminar until Dr. Victor Marco Emmanuel N. Ferriols was assigned as Project leader of Establishment of Genomics Consortium and Core Facility in Visayas (PGC VSF). Inception of Establishment of Genomics Consortium and Core Facility in Visayas (PGC VSF) took place in UP Visayas, Miag-ao, Iloilo last March 11-12, 2019 with the attendance of Dr. Benedict A. Maralit, the Director of PGC DSCF and representatives of consortium members. Approved anchor projects of PGC VSF as of November 2019 are “Transcriptome and gut metagenome profile of Penaeus vannamei exposed to fluctuating environmental temperatures” and “Profiling of transcriptome of seaweeds to elucidate “ice-ice” disease and epiphyte infestation mechanisms”.

In June 14, 2014, Dr. Cynthia P. Saloma, the PGC Executive Director, met with 21 potential consortium members and initiated brainstorming of five groups in seaweeds, giant freshwater prawn, freshwater areas monitoring, marine resource metabarcoding and biobanking, and drug discovery. As a DOST PCHRD-monitored project, PGC VSF has conducted the Genomics in Health Writeshop last June 24-25, 2019 which generated 4 submitted proposals on colorectal cancer, G6PD deficiency and diabetes mellitus from West Visayas State University College of Medicine (WVSU CM) and Corazon Locsin Montelibano Memorial Regional Hospital (CLMMRH).

content.rendered:
Philippine Genome Center Visayas is a research institution that serves as a hub for collaborative genomic research. Leveraging our strengths in genome sequencing, our mission is to advance genomic science, its application to agricultural and medical research, and spearhead the utilization of omics in fisheries and aquatic research in the Philippines. 

PGC Visayas provides a diverse array of services

content.rendered:
Opportunities

PGC Visayas provides trainings to students, researchers,
faculty members, and health professionals to improve their
skills involved in genome sequencing.

Students who wish to fully immerse themselves in genomics research can apply for the PGC Visayas Student Internship Program. It places a strong emphasis on knowledge dissemination, laboratory discovery, and career advancement

Currently, there are no job openings. To stay informed about future career opportunities, follow us on our social media accounts.

For tour information and to book a tour at PGC Visayas,
please send us an email at pgc.upvisayas@up.edu.ph.

Tours must be booked a minimum of two weeks in advance and address your letter to:

Victor Marco Emmanuel N. Ferriols, Ph.D.Assistant to the Executive Director – Visayas Philippine Genome Center
